heading: Transfer of consumer financial protection functions
label: 5581 Transfer of consumer financial protection functions

children.label: A
children.text: all authority to prescribe rules or issue orders or guidelines pursuant to any Federal consumer financial law, including performing appropriate functions to promulgate and review such rules, orders, and guidelines; and

children.label: B
children.text: the examination authority described in subsection (c)(1), with respect to a person described in ; andsection 5515(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the Board of Governors (and any Federal reserve bank, as the context requires), the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation, the Federal Trade Commission, the National Credit Union Administration, the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, the Office of Thrift Supervision, and the Department of Housing and Urban Development, and the heads of those agencies; and

children.label: B
children.text: the agencies listed in subparagraph (A), collectively.

children.heading: Transfer of functions
children.label: A Transfer of functions
children.text: All consumer financial protection functions of the Board of Governors are transferred to the Bureau.

children.heading: Board of Governors authority
children.label: B Board of Governors authority
children.text: The Bureau shall have all powers and duties that were vested in the Board of Governors, relating to consumer financial protection functions, on the day before the designated transfer date.

children.heading: Transfer of functions
children.label: A Transfer of functions
children.text: All consumer financial protection functions of the Comptroller of the Currency are transferred to the Bureau.

children.heading: Comptroller authority
children.label: B Comptroller authority
children.text: The Bureau shall have all powers and duties that were vested in the Comptroller of the Currency, relating to consumer financial protection functions, on the day before the designated transfer date.

children.heading: Director of the Office of Thrift Supervision
children.label: 3 Director of the Office of Thrift Supervision

children.heading: Transfer of functions
children.label: A Transfer of functions
children.text: All consumer financial protection functions of the Director of the Office of Thrift Supervision are transferred to the Bureau.

children.heading: Director authority
children.label: B Director authority
children.text: The Bureau shall have all powers and duties that were vested in the Director of the Office of Thrift Supervision, relating to consumer financial protection functions, on the day before the designated transfer date.

children.heading: Transfer of functions
children.label: A Transfer of functions
children.text: All consumer financial protection functions of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation are transferred to the Bureau.

children.heading: Corporation authority
children.label: B Corporation authority
children.text: The Bureau shall have all powers and duties that were vested in the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation, relating to consumer financial protection functions, on the day before the designated transfer date.

children.heading: Transfer of functions
children.label: A Transfer of functions
children.text: The authority of the Federal Trade Commission under an enumerated consumer law to prescribe rules, issue guidelines, or conduct a study or issue a report mandated under such law shall be transferred to the Bureau on the designated transfer date. Nothing in this title shall be construed to require a mandatory transfer of any employee of the Federal Trade Commission.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Bureau shall have all powers and duties under the enumerated consumer laws to prescribe rules, issue guidelines, or to conduct studies or issue reports mandated by such laws, that were vested in the Federal Trade Commission on the day before the designated transfer date.

children.heading: Federal Trade Commission Act
children.label: ii Federal Trade Commission Act
children.text: Subject to part B, the Bureau may enforce a rule prescribed under the Federal Trade Commission Act [ et seq.] by the Federal Trade Commission with respect to an unfair or deceptive act or practice to the extent that such rule applies to a covered person or service provider with respect to the offering or provision of a consumer financial product or service as if it were a rule prescribed under .15 U.S.C. 41section 5531 of this title

children.heading: Authority of the Federal Trade Commission
children.label: C Authority of the Federal Trade Commission

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: No provision of this title shall be construed as modifying, limiting, or otherwise affecting the authority of the Federal Trade Commission (including its authority with respect to affiliates described in ) under the Federal Trade Commission Act or any other law, other than the authority under an enumerated consumer law to prescribe rules, issue official guidelines, or conduct a study or issue a report mandated under such law.1section 5515(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Commission authority relating to rules prescribed by the Bureau
children.label: ii Commission authority relating to rules prescribed by the Bureau
children.text: Subject to part B, the Federal Trade Commission shall have authority to enforce under the Federal Trade Commission Act ( et seq.) a rule prescribed by the Bureau under this title with respect to a covered person subject to the jurisdiction of the Federal Trade Commission under that Act, and a violation of such a rule by such a person shall be treated as a violation of a rule issued under section 18 of that Act () with respect to unfair or deceptive acts or practices.15 U.S.C. 4115 U.S.C. 57a1

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: D Coordination
children.text: To avoid duplication of or conflict between rules prescribed by the Bureau under and the Federal Trade Commission under section 18(a)(1)(B) of the Federal Trade Commission Act [] that apply to a covered person or service provider with respect to the offering or provision of consumer financial products or services, the agencies shall negotiate an agreement with respect to rulemaking by each agency, including consultation with the other agency prior to proposing a rule and during the comment period.section 5531 of this title15 U.S.C. 57a(a)(1)(B)

children.label: i
children.text: Federal Trade Commission in making determinations regarding the meaning or interpretation of any provision of the Federal Trade Commission Act, or of any other Federal law for which the Commission has authority to prescribe rules; or

children.label: ii
children.text: Bureau in making determinations regarding the meaning or interpretation of any provision of a Federal consumer financial law (other than any law described in clause (i)).

children.heading: Transfer of functions
children.label: A Transfer of functions
children.text: All consumer financial protection functions of the National Credit Union Administration are transferred to the Bureau.

children.heading: National Credit Union Administration authority
children.label: B National Credit Union Administration authority
children.text: The Bureau shall have all powers and duties that were vested in the National Credit Union Administration, relating to consumer financial protection functions, on the day before the designated transfer date.

children.heading: Department of Housing and Urban Development
children.label: 7 Department of Housing and Urban Development

children.heading: Transfer of functions
children.label: A Transfer of functions
children.text: All consumer protection functions of the Secretary of the Department of Housing and Urban Development relating to the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act of 1974 ( et seq.), the Secure and Fair Enforcement for Mortgage Licensing Act of 2008 ( [5101] et seq.), and the Interstate Land Sales Full Disclosure Act ( et seq.) are transferred to the Bureau.12 U.S.C. 260112 U.S.C. 510215 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Authority of the Department of Housing and Urban Development
children.label: B Authority of the Department of Housing and Urban Development
children.text: The Bureau shall have all powers and duties that were vested in the Secretary of the Department of Housing and Urban Development relating to the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act of 1974 ( et seq.), the Secure and Fair Enforcement for Mortgage Licensing Act of 2008 ( et seq.), and the Interstate Land Sales Full Disclosure Act ( et seq.), on the day before the designated transfer date.12 U.S.C. 260112 U.S.C. 510115 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Authorities of the prudential regulators
children.label: c Authorities of the prudential regulators
A
children.text: authority to require reports from and conduct examinations for compliance with Federal consumer financial laws with respect to a person described in , that is incidental to the backup and enforcement procedures provided to the regulator under ; andsection 5515(a) of this titlesection 5515(c) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: exclusive authority (relative to the Bureau) to require reports from and conduct examinations for compliance with Federal consumer financial laws with respect to a person described in , except as provided to the Bureau under subsections (b) and (c) of .section 5516(a) of this titlesection 5516 of this title

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: A Limitation
children.text: The authority of a transferor agency that is a prudential regulator to enforce compliance with Federal consumer financial laws with respect to a person described in , shall be limited to the backup and enforcement procedures in described in .section 5515(a) of this title2section 5515(c) of this titleSo in original. The word “in” probably should not appear.2

children.heading: Exclusive authority
children.label: B Exclusive authority
children.text: A transferor agency that is a prudential regulator shall have exclusive authority (relative to the Bureau) to enforce compliance with Federal consumer financial laws with respect to a person described in , except as provided to the Bureau under subsections (b) and (c) of .section 5516(a) of this titlesection 5516 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the Federal Credit Union Act ( et seq.), by the National Credit Union Administration Board with respect to any covered person or service provider that is an insured credit union, or service provider thereto, or any affiliate of an insured credit union, who is subject to the jurisdiction of the Board under that Act; and12 U.S.C. 1751

children.label: ii
children.text: , by the appropriate Federal banking agency, as defined in , with respect to a covered person or service provider that is a person described in and who is subject to the jurisdiction of that agency, as set forth in sections 1813(q) and 1818 of this title; orsection 1818 of this titlesection 1813(q) of this titlesection 1813(q) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: the Bank Service Company Act ( et seq.).12 U.S.C. 1861

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: d Effective date
children.text: Subsections (b) and (c) shall become effective on the designated transfer date.
